body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.64-30 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @joekaufmanexpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777571394923859) ## Action Performed: Case 1 steps: 1. Login to an account with a company card feed. 2. Go to the workspace and invite a new user with an unvalidated account. 3. Go to Company cards and assign the unvalidated user a card. 4. Tap their card row. Case 2 steps: 1. Login to an account with an Expensify Card feed. 2. Go to the workspace and invite a new user with an unvalidated account. 3. Go to Expensify Card page and issue a card to the unvalidated user. 4. Tap their card row. ## Expected Result: In both cases, we don't need to show the admin a warning about the card being pending because of the unvalidated account. We already immediately scrape third-party cards when a card is assigned to an unvalidated user, so the current warning is incorrect. For Expensify Cards, we already immediately create the card, and the user must enter a magic code to view virtual card details or order a physical card, effectively validating their account. ## Actual Result: We show admins a warning on the card details page indicating the card is in a special pending state because the account is unvalidated, which is not the case. # Note: We show this same warning when importing a personal card to an unvalidated account in NewDot. Let's remove it there too ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5dfeedeb-77d8-4e9e-a5c3-abfb49a8e551 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.64-30 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @joekaufmanexpensify **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777571454366189) ## Action Performed: Case 1 steps: 1. Issue a physical Expensify Card to an unvalidated account 2. Go to Home and note the account is pending validation. 3. Go to Account > Wallet. 4. Tap the Expensify Card row. 5. Fill out details to order the card and note you're prompted for a magic code. 6. Enter the magic code and try to proceed. Case 2 steps: 1. Issue a virtual Expensify Card to an unvalidated account. 2. Go to Home and note the account is pending validation. 3. Go to Account > Wallet. 4. Tap the Expensify Card row. 5. Try to view virtual card details and note you're prompted for a magic code. 6. Enter the magic code and try to proceed. ## Expected Result: Case 1: Successfully entering the magic code to order the physical card validates the account and then orders the card, rather than forcing the user to go validate the account elsewhere. Case 2: Successfully entering magic code to view virtual card details validates the account and then reveals the card details. ## Actual Result: Case 1: Ordering a physical card with an unvalidated account silently fails. API command returns an error for the unvalidated account. [Logs for failure](https://www.expensify.com/_devportal/tools/logSearch/#sort=asc&size=10000&query=request_id%3A%3D%229f484eb71811983e-EWR%22%20timestamp%3A%5B2026-04-30T00%3A00%20TO%202026-05-01T23%3A59%5D&engine=victorialogs). Case 2: You're able to view the virtual card details after entering a magic code with an unvalidated account, but doing so does not validate the account. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f8936bdb-8af2-46d5-bd94-d6d02d60c59f [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Don't fire duplicate ReconnectApp on delegate account switch

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
This issue tracks the fix for duplicate `ReconnectApp` requests being fired when switching accounts via delegate access. When a user switches accounts via delegate access, the app sends `OpenApp` and then an unnecessary `ReconnectApp` right after. The `OpenApp` already performs a full sync, making the subsequent `ReconnectApp` wasted work. **PR:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/89262 --- cc @mountiny @TMisiukiewicz @abzokhattab

title: [$250] Fix freeze when opening "Add approval workflow" on workspaces with many members

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
This issue tracks the fix for a performance freeze when opening "Add approval workflow" on workspaces with many members. **Problem:** `getMemberAccountIDsForWorkspace(policy?.employeeList)` was being called inside two separate `.map()` callbacks, creating O(N²) work. On workspaces with a few hundred members, pressing "Add approval workflow" froze the app for roughly a minute. **Fix PR:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/89268 The fix hoists the call to the component body so it runs once per render instead of once per member.

title: [$250] Fix doubled OpenApp on delegate (copilot) account switch

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
When a user switches into a copilot/delegate account via the account switcher, `OpenApp` fires twice — the first call resolves with skeletons visible, then a second `OpenApp` runs while skeletons are still visible, causing a noticeable double-loading experience on large accounts. **Two independent root causes:** 1. **NetworkState reconfigure path**: The `CONNECT_AS_DELEGATE` response updates `SESSION.accountID`. The SESSION subscriber in `NetworkState.ts` calls `configureAndSubscribe()` to rebuild the NetInfo subscription. Re-subscribing emits a synthetic `null → true` transition that the listener treats as a network recovery event → `reconnect()` → `openApp()`. 2. **DelegateAccessHandler recovery effect path**: `Onyx.clear(KEYS_TO_PRESERVE_DELEGATE_ACCESS)` preserves `HAS_LOADED_APP=true` but not `IS_LOADING_APP`. After the clear, the state is briefly `hasLoadedApp=true && isLoadingApp=undefined` — exactly the trigger for the recovery effect, which fires its own `openApp()`. **Related PR:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/89254
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @abzokhattab

  • Upwork Job URL: https://www.upwork.com/jobs/~022049837260593240936
  • Upwork Job ID: 2049837260593240936

title: [$250] New Expensify often appears offline on stable network connections in Safari

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
### Problem I mostly use the Safari PWA for New Expensify, and it almost always shows me as offline, even when I have a stable connection and everything else is normal. This happens if I use Safari directly as well. More notes: - The problem seems to be that ~80-90% of `Ping` requests fail (after ~10s timeout) Given that Safari is the 2nd-most-popular browser (~15% market share for desktop browsers), we should fix this. ### Solution Still trying to nail down the root cause. It's not the root cause, but one thing we should probably do regardless to reduce unnecessary network traffic is to implement `shouldReachabilityRun` such that we don't send `Ping` if we already received a success response from the same domain `expensify.com` in the normal ping period.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Pujan92

Both accounts have workspace. Account 1: logged in NewDot and own worksapce. Account 2: logged in NewDot and own worksapce. New user A: email with applause.expensifail.com domain and no login Expensify before (unvalidated.) 1. Account 1: go to workspace members and invite New user A. 2. Account 2: go to workspace members and invite New user A. ## Expected Result: Account 1 and Account 2: New user A is added to worksapce without error. ## Actual Result: Account 1: New user A is added to worksapce without error. Account 2: An error appears 'There was a problem adding this workspace member' ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b21a7e0-5205-4654-bcbd-e41f176c769c [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** v9.3.63-0 (web) v9.3.59-4 (mobile, iOS) **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @marianaabussamra **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777385657113769) ## Action Performed: 1. Navigate to Account → Wallet 2. Click on an existing bank account 3. Click “Continue setup” 4. Observe: Chat message: “Our team will review your information and circle back” ## Expected Result: One of the following should happen: If submitted: Bank account should be fully in review state No prompt to continue setup Concierge message or tracking (e.g., internal follow-up) should exist If incomplete: User should be clearly prompted to complete missing information No “under review” messaging should appear ## Actual Result: User sees: Message indicating review in progress Simultaneous prompt to continue setup Backend (via Supportal) shows: Incomplete bank account data No: Concierge follow-up Clear status tracking ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshot for the issue is in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1777385657113769) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Decompose MoneyRequestConfirmationListFooter into field groups and hooks

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
`MoneyRequestConfirmationListFooter` has grown to ~850 lines, mixing the orchestration of 13 fields, derived-flag computation, receipt-source resolution, and compact-mode layout into a single component. This makes the file difficult to maintain, test in isolation, and reason about. We should extract the logic into focused field groups and hooks: - **`ConfirmationFieldList`** — slim orchestrator owning the "Show more" button + Sparkles header - **`fieldGroups/TransactionDetailsFields`** — Amount, Description, Distance, Rate, Merchant, Time - **`fieldGroups/ClassificationFields`** — Category, Date, Tags, Tax, Attendees - **`fieldGroups/SettingsFields`** — Toggles + ReportField; gates `ReportField`'s 5 Onyx subscriptions behind `isPolicyExpenseChat` - **`hooks/useFooterDerivedFlags`** — derived flag chain pulled out of the footer body - **`hooks/useReceiptThumbnailSource`** — receipt-source resolution + first-render capture (Compiler-compliant `useState` pattern) - **`hooks/useFooterTagVisibility`**, **`hooks/useCompactReceiptDimensions`** — small isolated derivations - **`ConfirmationReceiptThumbnail`** — receipt area as a dedicated subtree This is a pure refactor with no behavioral changes. The confirmation screen should render all fields identically before and after. **Related:** Part of the broader performance effort in https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/81849
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ShridharGoel

title: [$250] Expose Accounting saved searches to Auditors

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
**Problem:** Currently in NewDot we only display Accounting saved searches to Admins. However, these are also useful reports for users with Auditor (i.e. read only) permission. Given these users can already see all the reports on the workspace they are an Auditor for, there is no reason they cannot also see the Accounting saved searches. **Solution:** Expose Accounting saved searches to Auditors cc @HaleyJacobson @kortneywynn @JmillsExpensify
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @mananjadhav

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.62-8 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://test-management.browserstack.com/projects/2219752/test-runs/TR-22585/folder/13176890/41237378/1371128462 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+bm1010@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Web, android **App Component:** Workspace Settings ## Action Performed: Preconditions: Issue a virtual Expensify card to yourself. Add a copilot with any access level to the account. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Log in as the copilot. 3. Switch to the admin's account via the account switcher. 4. Navigate to the Workspace chat containing the system message about the issued card. ## Expected Result: "card" in the system message should be hyperlinked. ## Actual Result: "Card" in the system message is displayed as plain text and is not hyperlinked when viewed by a copilot. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2468e9a4-b965-4969-bc40-a50b067c83c5 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
